fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of the production order.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the production order.

fields.name: Search Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the search description.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the production order description.

fields.name: Creation Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which you created the production order.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the production order card was last modified.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source type of the production order.

fields.name: Source No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item number or number of the source document that the entry originates from.

fields.name: Routing No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the routing number used for this production order.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the source document that the entry originates from.

fields.name: Inventory Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies links between business transactions made for the item and an inventory account in the general ledger, to group amounts for that item type.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether there is a comment for this production order.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Prod. Order, and then click Comments. A window appears that displays existing comments and allows you to create new ones.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox. Notes follow documents when they are posted in the same way as comments.
The program automatically returns the entry to the Comment field if there is an entry in the Comments window.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.

fields.name: Starting Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting time of the production order.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the production order.

fields.name: Ending Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending time of the production order.

fields.name: Ending Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date of the production order.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the due date of the production order.

fields.name: Finished Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the actual finishing date of a finished production order.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the posting of consumption and output transactions for the released production order is blocked.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location code to which you want to post the finished product from this production order.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a bin to which you want to post the finished items.

fields.name: Replan Ref. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the production order that was replanned.

fields.name: Replan Ref. Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of the production order that was replanned.

fields.name: Low-Level Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the low-level code of the item you want to produce with this production order.
The low-level code is the lowest level on which the item resides in all bills of material. Every part has only one low-level code. If, for example, the BOM is a component of a second BOM, which in turn is a component of a third BOM, the field will contain 2 because the item is a component of a BOM at the third level in the BOM structure. (The top level is designated by 0.)

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item or the family to produce (production quantity).

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the unit cost of the item to produce if the Source Type is item.
The system copies the unit costs from the Unit Cost field on the item card. You can change the unit costs manually.

fields.name: Cost Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total unit costs.
These are calculated as follows:
Cost Amount = Quantity x Unit Cost.

fields.name: Work Center Filter
fields.tooltip:
Displays the filter that filters by work center.
If the field contains a work center code, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries with the work center that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

Packing department

Entries from the packing department work center


Interval

1..5

Entries from work center 1 through 5


Either/or

Packing department / Machine department

Entries that are from either packing department or machine department


Different from

<>1

Entries from all work centers except work center 1


To see the work center codes in the Work Center table, click the field.

fields.name: Capacity Type Filter
fields.tooltip:
Shows the filter that filters by capacity type.
If the field contains a work center code, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries for work centers. You can choose work center or machine center to define a filter.

fields.name: Capacity No. Filter
fields.tooltip:
Shows the filter that filters by work center or machine center, depending on the entry in the Capacity Type Filter field.
If the field contains a work center code, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries for the work center that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:


Meaning


Sample Expression


Included


Equal to

Packing department

Entries from the packing department work center


Interval

1..5

Entries from work center 1 through 5


Either/or

Packing department / Machine department

Entries that are from either packing department or machine department


Different from

<>1

Entries from all work centers except work center 1

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the value in the Allocated Capacity Need field shows the allocated capacity need for a production order for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Expected Operation Cost Amt.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected operation costs amount.
The program automatically calculates the costs expected to produce the item.
The program calculates the expected operation cost amount by the entries in the table Prod. Order Routing Line in the Expected Operation Cost Amt. field

fields.name: Expected Component Cost Amt.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected component cost amount.
The program automatically calculates the costs expected for the components in this production order.
The field contents are displayed depending on the entry in the Date Filter field.
The program calculates the expected component cost amount based on the entries in the table Prod. Order Components in the Cost Amount field.

fields.name: Actual Time Used
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the actual time used.
The program automatically calculates the actual time used for this production order by the entries in the Capacity Ledger Entry table in the Quantity field.
If there is an entry in the Date Filter field, the actual time used are calculated within this date filter.

fields.name: Allocated Capacity Need
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the allocated capacity need.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Needed Time field in the Prod. Order Capacity Need table.
You can filter the allocated capacity need field in order that the contents are calculated only on the base of certain dates, capacity types, capacity numbers and/or work centers.
To see the prod order capacity need entries that make up the quantity shown, click the field.

fields.name: Expected Capacity Need
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected capacity need.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Needed Time field in the Prod. Order Capacity Need table.
You can filter the expected capacity need field in order that the contents are calculated only on the base of certain dates, capacity types, capacity numbers and/or work centers.
To see the production order capacity need entries that make up the quantity shown, click the field.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number series that was used when this production order was created.
It is possible to create different number series, one of these number series can be selected in the Manufacturing Setup to determine which number series is to be used to create production orders.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.

fields.name: Planned Order No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the planned production order or the number that the order had during the planned status.
If a production order is or was created directly with the firm planned status, this field can be empty.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.

fields.name: Firm Planned Order No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the firm planned production order or the number that the order had during the firm planned status.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.

fields.name: Simulated Order No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the simulated production order or the number that the order had during the simulated status.
You cannot edit or delete the contents of this field.

fields.name: Expected Material Ovhd. Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected material overhead costs for this production order.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Overhead Amount field in the Prod. Order Component table.
To see the Production Order Component Entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Expected Capacity Ovhd. Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected capacity overhead cost for this production order.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Expected Capacity Ovhd. Cost field in the Prod. Order Routing Line table.
To see the Production Order Routing Line Entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Starting Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date and starting time of the production order.

fields.name: Ending Date-Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date and ending time of the production order.

fields.name: Document Put-away Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of the warehouse put-away.

fields.name: Reopened
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the production order is reopened.

fields.name: Manual Scheduling
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the End/Due Dates on the production have been scheduled manually.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Completely Picked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether all production components have been completely picked.

fields.name: Assigned User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.
